channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] STATEMENT FROM JERSEY1ST, NEW JERSEY POLICY INSTITUTE, AND AMERICANS FOR PROSPERITY–NEW JERSEY ON NEW WORKER CLASSIFICATION RULE
item.description:
For Immediate Release: May 6, 2026 TRENTON, N.J. – In response to Governor Sherrill’s adoption of new worker classification regulations tied […]

The post STATEMENT FROM JERSEY1ST, NEW JERSEY POLICY INSTITUTE, AND AMERICANS FOR PROSPERITY–NEW JERSEY ON NEW WORKER CLASSIFICATION RULE first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] The Big, Beautiful Bill is law. Now Arkansas has to get it right.
item.description:
Passed almost one year ago, the famous “One Big Beautiful Bill” contains the most transformational welfare reforms in modern history. […]

The post The Big, Beautiful Bill is law. Now Arkansas has to get it right. first appeared on Opportunity Arkansas.

item.title: [Empowerms] Josh McManus: Jackson’s Comeback Story
item.description:
Josh McManus: Jackson’s Comeback Story What does it take for a struggling city to turn the corner? In this episode of The Empower Podcast, Grant Callen sits down with urban strategist and entrepreneur Josh McManus to discuss the rise, decline, and revitalization of American cities, and why he believes Jackson still has enormous potential. Drawing…

The post Josh McManus: Jackson’s Comeback Story first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] The Myth of the Moral Vacuum: Why Every Law is a Moral Choice
item.description:
When the debate over vice laws, those governing drugs, gambling, or pornography, reaches the halls of our legislature, a familiar, hollow cry rings out: You can’t legislate morality! While it might be a catchy slogan (oft repeated by the vice peddlers themselves), it is designed to end a conversation before it begins. It suggests that […]

The post The Myth of the Moral Vacuum: Why Every Law is a Moral Choice appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] Venti-Sized Subsidy
item.description: Tennessee taxpayers are about to spend millions on a Starbucks order they didn’t ask for. In April, the world’s largest coffee chain announced it would be opening a Southeast corporate office in Music City.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Billionaires Are Not The Problem
item.description:
“You can’t earn a billion dollars. You just can’t earn that. You can get market power. You can break rules. You can do all sorts of things. You can abuse labor laws. You can pay people less than what they’re worth. But you can’t earn that.” These comments made by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez on a […]

The post Billionaires Are Not The Problem appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Play for the Team on Your Jersey: It’s an Integrity Thing.
item.description: Imagine your favorite sports team. They all wear the same uniform, stand under the same banner, and operate under the same creed — not just to play, but to win according to a shared playbook. Each player brings unique strengths, but when they join the team, they all commit to the same rules, values, and […]

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] The Defeat of Measure 120 is a Win for the Oregon System
item.description:
By Naomi Inman It’s election time in Oregon and the Oregonian editorial board...

The post The Defeat of Measure 120 is a Win for the Oregon System appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Great Nations Aren't Destroyed by Enemies. They're Destroyed by Debt.
item.description:
Imagine your family spent more this year than you earned. It wouldn't be comfortable, but you'd manage. Now imagine your family had spent more than it earned every single year since 2001. By now you'd be destitute. Well, that is exactly what the federal government has been doing. For the past 25 years — ever […]

The post Great Nations Aren't Destroyed by Enemies. They're Destroyed by Debt. appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Manchester school board elevates educator staffing above all other priorities
item.description:
With Manchester’s school district looking to trim its proposed 2027 budget to stay within the city’s tax cap, the Board of School Committee took the extraordinary step of forbidding the district from achieving a balanced budget by laying off educators. The move effectively elevates raw educator staffing numbers over all other district priorities, even though […]

The post Manchester school board elevates educator staffing above all other priorities appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Beacontn] Beacon Applauds Department of Labor Decision to Withdraw Unlawful Overtime Rule
item.description: Today, the U.S. Department of Labor will formally publish a final rule overturning the Biden administration’s 2024 overtime rule after multiple federal courts vacated the rule nationwide and the department declined to continue defending it on appeal.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF talks to KOAT 7: What ever happened to taxpayers-subsidized Unser Crossing?
item.description:
New Mexico has a long history of spending taxpayer dollars on failed developments that don’t pan out. Going back nearly 20 years to the Unser Crossing development (originally slated for Unser and Central) was one such plan. Of course, RGF loves economic development. We want to see jobs and businesses locate to our state, but […]

The post RGF talks to KOAT 7: What ever happened to taxpayers-subsidized Unser Crossing? appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Foropportunity] How Georgia’s Governor Impacts Your Daily Life: Understanding the Role and the Election Process
item.description:
From vetoing bills to deciding the budget, check out five ways Georgia’s governor impacts schools, safety, work, and public programs that our families and communities rely on.

The post How Georgia’s Governor Impacts Your Daily Life: Understanding the Role and the Election Process appeared first on For Opportunity.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Unleashing Hope via K–12 Scholarships for Families: Why Rhode Island Should Opt-In to Federal Education Tax Credits
item.description:
Rhode Island students deserve better. The federal Education Freedom Tax Credit (EFTC) empowers working and middle-class families with scholarships for K-12 private schools, charters, homeschooling, tutoring & more at zero cost to RI taxpayers or public schools. The governor can opt-in today for January 2027 launch. Give Ocean State families real choice and brighter futures. Don’t leave millions in education funding on the table.

The post Unleashing Hope via K–12 Scholarships for Families: Why Rhode Island Should Opt-In to Federal Education Tax Credits appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Empirecenter] New York Schools will Spend More and Deliver Less – Again
item.description:
As voters across New York head to the polls to decide their local school budgets, the big question – why....

The post New York Schools will Spend More and Deliver Less – Again appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] The Property Tax: Expensive and Outdated
item.description: When my dog gets hold of one of her toys, she won’t let it go. The harder I try to take it, the harder she holds on. I’m the same way about the revolutionary idea of eliminating property taxes in Idaho. The more you try to talk me out of abolishing these unfair, misguided, and […]

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] SURPLUS REVENUES FUEL INCOME TAX CUTS
item.description: (May 2026) One sentence in an annual state report explains how policymakers have achieved a serious fiscal goal: reduction of the top state individual income tax rate under three governors and a succession of Republican legislatures. “(Arkansas) revenues are expanding because the business cycle’s current state is expansion, not contraction.” Policy Foundation research memo, Nov. […]

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Pioneer Institute Study Calls for Attrition Strategy to Rein in Record-High Massachusetts State Workforce
item.description:
BOSTON – The size of the state workforce reached an all-time high in 2025, and a new Pioneer Institute study recommends implementing an attrition program that would reduce the number of positions under the governor’s authority by 5,000 and save taxpayers $1.5 billion over five years by backfilling three of every four positions that are vacated.

The post Pioneer Institute Study Calls for Attrition Strategy to Rein in Record-High Massachusetts State Workforce appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] The Gig Is Up for the 2024 Independent Contractor Rule
item.description:
WILL submitted a comment letter to the U.S. Department of Labor in support of a reversing a Biden-era independent contractor rule which actively threatened the independence of workers and businesses by trapping them in an unpredictable regulatory guessing game.

The post The Gig Is Up for the 2024 Independent Contractor Rule appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] TOP ARKANSAS INCOME TAX RATE CUT TO 3.7%
item.description: (May 2026) Arkansas policymakers reduced the top state individual income tax rate from 3.9% to 3.7% in early May, using an estimated $334.4 million projected fiscal surplus in the current 2025-26 fiscal year. Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders called a special session of the state legislature April 30 to consider the tax cut. The session convened […]

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH offers the lowest gas and grocery prices in New England this Memorial Day Weekend
item.description:
Granite Staters can expect to have the most affordable Memorial Day Weekend in New England this year, as New Hampshire offers the region’s lowest gas and grocery prices, a Josiah Bartlett Center for Public Policy review of regional prices shows.  Memorial Day weekend kicks off both summer travel season and outdoor grilling season, and this […]

The post NH offers the lowest gas and grocery prices in New England this Memorial Day Weekend appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Building on the Wisconsin higher-ed reform model
item.description:
Wisconsin can pursue practical, targeted reforms that improve accountability, strengthen academic standards and better align universities with the needs of students and taxpayers without dismantling the university system itself.

The post Building on the Wisconsin higher-ed reform model appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] A good lesson in curtailing government growth
item.description:
Do not let it be said that I only criticize government officials. I also like to give credit where credit is due. This week, a substantial amount of credit and praise is owed to the Honolulu Budget Committee, which made the fiscally responsible decision to ax most of the funding for the city’s Office of […]

The post A good lesson in curtailing government growth appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Tallahassee Democrat: Levy Cap: Florida’s Smartest Option for Property Tax Reform
item.description:
Florida needs property tax reform that is sustainable and honest about trade-offs. Of the options now under consideration,...

The post Tallahassee Democrat: Levy Cap: Florida’s Smartest Option for Property Tax Reform appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Believe it or not, Washington Once Had Fiscal Restraint
item.description:
A century ago, Presidents Warren G. Harding and Calvin Coolidge made balancing budgets, reducing debt, and limiting government growth national priorities. Their administrations did not just slow spending growth — they actually cut federal spending, reduced the national debt, lowered tax rates, and produced budget surpluses. Fiscal restraint was politically difficult then, just as it is today, but the Harding and Coolidge years demonstrate that disciplined budgeting and strong economic growth can coexist.

The post Believe it or not, Washington Once Had Fiscal Restraint appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Empowerms] Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again?
item.description:
Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again? On this episode of the Empower Podcast, State Rep. Otis Anthony joins Grant for a wide-ranging conversation about poverty, work, education, economic development, and the future of the Mississippi Delta. Anthony shares how growing up in Indianola shaped his view of public service, why he believes the Delta’s…

The post Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again? first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Libertas] Libertas Network Acquires Family Brand to Strengthen Intentional Families
item.description:
Libertas Network’s acquisition of Praxis ignites a bold revolution in education by empowering young innovators to bypass outdated systems and chart their own path to success.

The post Libertas Network Acquires Family Brand to Strengthen Intentional Families appeared first on Libertas Network: Changing Hearts, Minds, and Laws.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas Should Welcome AI Growth, Not Zone It Away
item.description: Artificial intelligence is not floating in the clouds. It runs on land, energy, fiber, water systems, skilled workers, and data centers. That means Kansas has a choice. It can become a place where the next generation of digital infrastructure is built, or it can regulate, zone, and subsidize its way into mediocrity while investment goes […]

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Pioneer Institute Applauds Healey Administration’s Proposal to Reduce LLC Filing Fees; More Reforms Needed to Address Economic Malaise
item.description:
Pioneer Institute applauds the Healey Administration’s proposal to reduce the LLC filing fee from $500 to $100 and discount annual LLC fees during the early years of a new business. These changes remove two unnecessary barriers facing entrepreneurs and small business owners looking to launch and grow in Massachusetts.
We believe that it is crucial that state leaders build on these proposals to address the Commonwealth’s economic malaise, which started with the pandemic.

The post Pioneer Institute Applauds Healey Administration’s Proposal to Reduce LLC Filing Fees; More Reforms Needed to Address Economic Malaise appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Attorney General Mike Hilgers on How We Grow Nebraska
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Nebraska’s Attorney General, Mike Hilgers. During the episode Attorney General Hilgers and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss the the importance of good energy policy in Nebraska, how we attract new residents and investment, and the next big issues that will shape the future of...

The post Nebraskanomics: Attorney General Mike Hilgers on How We Grow Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Backs HUD Plan to Let Public Housing Add Work Requirements
item.description:
WILL filed a public comment in support of a new Housing and Urban Development (HUD) rule that would let public housing agencies and landlords require able-bodied adults receiving housing assistance to work up to 40 hours per week and limit assistance to two years.

The post WILL Backs HUD Plan to Let Public Housing Add Work Requirements appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation launches website to educate public employees about union alternatives
item.description:
EmpoweredEmployees.org gives workers clear information on how to remove or replace unwanted labor unions.

The post Freedom Foundation launches website to educate public employees about union alternatives appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] When City Councils Play Secretary of State
item.description:
In the years since the National Civic League published “Is It None of Our Business?,” the trend of city halls tackling national issues has shifted from a novelty to a standard operating model. The League’s 2023 analysis argued that local agencies should embrace a broader national agenda—a shift they championed as a positive phenomenon. However, from...

The post When City Councils Play Secretary of State first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.description:
May 27, 2026, 8:30 a.m. Historic County Building To: Kaua'i County Council Arryl Kaneshiro, Chair KipuKai Kuali'i, Vice Chair From: Grassroot Institute of Hawaii Jonathan Helton, Policy Analyst RE: Bill 2995 — RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAX Aloha Chair Kaneshiro, Vice Chair Kuali'i and other Committee members, The Grassroot Institute of Hawaii offers comments on — […]

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Good riddance to non-use of public lands
item.description: The rule rescission brings common sense back to federal land management while also protecting the livelihoods of thousands who responsibly make a living on our shared inheritance.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] CALIFORNIA: IHSS providers tricked into staying, overpaying
item.description:

item.title: [Jamesmadison] AICOA Would Pull Up the Ladder on Florida’s Small Businesses
item.description:
Washington didn’t build Florida’s 3.5 million small businesses. Low taxes, light regulation, and an entrepreneurial culture built an economy where 3.8 million Floridians...

item.title: [Mspolicy] UPSKILL: Putting the state's needs first, avoiding big questions
item.description:
Lawmakers across the country often insist on making “investments” in economic programs to help fill a perceived need for skilled workers. But such efforts often put the needs of the state and favored industries ahead of the needs of individuals -- and they miss big picture questions in the process.  This critical view should be […]

The post UPSKILL: Putting the state's needs first, avoiding big questions appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

While Sen. Martin Heinrich and the Rio Grande […]

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Restoring accountability in Wisconsin government
item.description:
Wisconsin’ recent failures of accountability share a common feature: Power has increasingly shifted away from the Legislature and toward governors, agencies, courts and other institutions that operate further from direct democratic control.

The post Restoring accountability in Wisconsin government appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF President discusses AI data centers on “Powering America” podcast
item.description:
In the latest episode of the Powering America Podcast, Paul Gessing, president of the Rio Grande Foundation, discusses data centers and energy policy in New Mexico. Gessing highlights the state’s potential for data center development due to its natural resources and stable environment, while also addressing the challenges posed by political opposition and environmental concerns. The […]

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Teacher Unions Are Weakening Nationally. Connecticut Remains Deeply Committed
item.description:
Every spring, Connecticut taxpayers encounter the same school-budget ritual. School spending goes up. Officials cite fixed costs. When towns look for savings, the public conversation turns quickly to larger classes, […]

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] The Data On Data Centers
item.description:
Data centers have existed for years, with the first modern data storage and computing facility dating back to the 1940s. For the most part, these components of our digital infrastructure took a backseat to flashier developments. With the proliferation of artificial intelligence (AI) and America’s increased economic and technological independence on this technology, data centers...

The post The Data On Data Centers appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Report: Wisconsin public schools have record staffing, declining enrollment
item.description:
Report: Wisconsin public schools have record staffing, declining enrollment (The Center Square) – Wisconsin public schools broke a record with the most staff ever employed in the state this year with 113,171 despite decreasing student enrollment, according to a new report from the Institute for Reforming Government. The state educates 791,794 students in public schools, […]

The post Report: Wisconsin public schools have record staffing, declining enrollment appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Muslim Sensitivities “Tolerance is the virtue of the man without convictions.” — G.K. Chesterton WE HAVE WAITED long enough for someone in the GOP leadership to defend the lieutenant governor’s right to describe Islam as “demonic.” That none has done so says a lot about the Indiana Republican Party. The Council on American-Islamic Relations took immediate umbrage and Read the full article...

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Lawmakers choose innovation over red tape
item.description:
What if we had another way to travel interisland? Something cheaper and more environmentally friendly than the usual airport-to-airport grind? No, I’m not talking about resurrecting the Hawaii Superferry. There’s a new option on the horizon, and I’m excited to tell you that my colleagues at the Grassroot Institute of Hawaii played a small role […]

The post Lawmakers choose innovation over red tape appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Franke: Insanity Defense
item.description: by Mark Franke To listen to typical conversations these days, one can easily get the impression that most issues have simple answers. It seems that no matter the question at hand, the responses are quick in coming and declarative to the extreme. One friend of mine ends his strongly opinionated statements with “Period!”. That is Read the full article...

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Mountain West states climb into Top 10 for fiscal health
item.description: For Montana, Idaho, and Wyoming – the 2025 rankings reinforce the idea that fiscal conservatism is no longer simply a political slogan in the region, but a defining component of economic strategy and regional identity. It’s also a reminder for Washington of the important fiscal reforms needed.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] The History Behind Iowa’s Legislative Calendar
item.description:
Iowa has no constitutional or statutory limit on session length. The 100- and 110-day deadlines are informal benchmarks tied to per diem payments and staffing, not mandatory adjournment dates. The 2026 session lasted 112 days. Lawmakers worked beyond the traditional endpoint, finishing on May 3 after a marathon final push. Annual sessions have not always been the norm. Iowa moved from biennial to annual legislative sessions through a 1968 constitutional amendment, while still allowing lawmakers flexibility to extend their work when needed.

The post The History Behind Iowa’s Legislative Calendar appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.description:

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] John Dickinson and the case against Independence
item.description: July 1, 1776, witnessed one of the most consequential debates in American history. The topic was whether America should declare independence. The principal participants were the passionate John Adams of Massachusetts for the affirmative and the eloquent John Dickinson of Pennsylvania for the negative.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Texas is About to Spend $100 Billion on Power Lines It Does Not Need
item.description:
Our electric bills are about to go up. Most Texans have no idea why. Texas regulators approved $33 billion in new high-voltage power lines (the Strategic Transmission Expansion Plan, or STEP) without a single legislative vote. Factor in financing, maintenance, and taxes over the life of the projects, and the tab for ratepayers hits nearly...

The post Texas is About to Spend $100 Billion on Power Lines It Does Not Need first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.description:

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Artificial Intelligence Takes Hurricane Season by Storm
item.description:
Hurricane season began this week, on June 1, and stretches through November 30th. For Louisiana, the season has become part of routine life and its residents are no stranger to both the minor annoyances and major tragic upheavals that accompany the storms. Developments in artificial intelligence (AI) are rapidly changing the way states prepare for,...

item.title: [Beacontn] First Principles Series: Don’t Ban Data Centers. Don’t Subsidize Them, Either.
item.description: We can allow data centers to exist while keeping the courts open for nuisance lawsuits against bad actors who infringe upon protections like water rights when other property owners are actually harmed by them.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Is Iowa Law in Violation of the Takings Clause?
item.description:
Iowa's tax sale system allows private investors to acquire property when taxes go unpaid, raising questions about whether homeowners can lose equity that exceeds the amount of taxes owed. Both the Iowa and U.S. Constitutions protect property rights, and the Fifth Amendment's Takings Clause prohibits the government from taking private property without just compensation. A unanimous 2023 U.S. Supreme Court ruling held that government cannot keep proceeds beyond a taxpayer's debt, a precedent that may have significant implications for Iowa's tax sale laws.

The post Is Iowa Law in Violation of the Takings Clause? appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Unconstitutional Programs and the Administrative State: Stopping the 1419 Project
item.description: Over the past century, political progressives have built a bureaucratic machine with the goal of undermining our republic. It accomplishes this by making people dependent on government programs and by ignoring the will of the people and their elected representatives, ruling solely through executive fiat. Idaho has long been at the forefront of the battle […]

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Minnesota spends $57k per Medicaid enrollee with disabilities. Is this sustainable?
item.description:
According to data from the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS), in 2023, Minnesota spent $12,982 per average Medicaid enrollee. Only Pennsylvania and Washington exceeded this amount. For people with disabilities, Minnesota spent $57,388 per person, the highest level in the entire country. The median state spent $26,566, less than half as much. Minnesota spent...

The post Minnesota spends $57k per Medicaid enrollee with disabilities. Is this sustainable? appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] TPPF Releases New Research on ERCOT’s 765-kv Strategic Transmission Expansion Plan
item.description:
AUSTIN– Today, TPPF’s Life:Powered campaign released new research outlining the cost and impact of ERCOT’s 765kv Strategic Transmission Expansion Plan or STEP. The paper finds that the STEP does not meaningfully reduce the need for new generation, nor does it reduce prices for consumers.  In fact, the STEP would impose nearly $100 billion in lifetime...

The post TPPF Releases New Research on ERCOT’s 765-kv Strategic Transmission Expansion Plan first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Data Centers Can Bring Their Own Tax Cuts
item.description:
Listen to this article There is a saying in areas prone to significant flooding that “floods bring their own rain.” Like many legends and old wives’ tales, it isn’t scientifically […]

The post Data Centers Can Bring Their Own Tax Cuts appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] You’re Invited to the Freedom Gala: Freedom’s Next 250 Years
item.description:
You’re Invited to the Freedom Gala: Freedom’s Next 250 Years Join Goldwater for the 2026 Freedom Gala on October 23, 2026,..

The post You’re Invited to the Freedom Gala: Freedom’s Next 250 Years appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] A Win for Worker Freedom in Louisiana! SB312 Awaits Gov. Landry’s Signature
item.description:
A big win for worker freedom in Louisiana! Senate Bill 312, sponsored by Senator Kirk Talbot and Representative Mike Melerine, is on its way to Governor Jeff Landry’s desk. This bill gives public sector workers more control over their paychecks by: • Allowing union members to resign their union membership and stop paying dues at...

The post A Win for Worker Freedom in Louisiana! SB312 Awaits Gov. Landry’s Signature appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Mackinac] Electric school bus replacement plan makes you say watt?
item.description: Uncle Sam provides $125 million for replacement vehicles

item.title: [Pacificresearch] A public bank in California would be costly, risky and unnecessary
item.description:
But lawmakers were pushing forward anyway. AB 2243 would have established a taxpayer-funded commission to study the feasibility of a public bank and how it could act “as an additional financial tool to lower borrowing costs, strengthen local lending partnerships and help finance urgent public needs like affordable housing, infrastructure, climate resilience and small business […]

The post A public bank in California would be costly, risky and unnecessary  appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Illinois ranks in top 10 for state and local taxes
item.description:
The state’s residents paid over $8,300, which was 18.5 percent more than the national average.

The post Illinois ranks in top 10 for state and local taxes appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Eichenberger: The Victim Mentality
item.description: by Dan Eichenberger MD, MBA In every era, people face hardship. What changes is how societies respond. Ancient cultures — especially Greece, Rome and early Christianity — emphasized agency, resilience and personal responsibility. Today, many of our institutions promote the opposite: a victim mentality that externalizes blame, demands validation  and weakens character. History suggests that tough love — Read the full article...

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Pioneer Institute Urges Governor Healey to Bring Federal Scholarship Dollars to Massachusetts Families
item.description:
As states decide whether to participate in the new federal scholarship tax credit program, Pioneer Institute is urging Governor Healey to ensure Massachusetts families are not left behind.

In a letter to the governor, Executive Director Jim Stergios notes that governors from blue states, including most recently New York Governor Kathy Hochul, have already moved to participate in the program. Massachusetts students and families should not be denied the same opportunity.

The post Pioneer Institute Urges Governor Healey to Bring Federal Scholarship Dollars to Massachusetts Families appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Germany’s Freeloader Drug Policy
item.description:
Germany is becoming a freeloader in global pharmaceutical innovation. American patients and taxpayers finance a disproportionate share of the research..

The post Germany’s Freeloader Drug Policy appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Chris Ingstad on Iowa’s 2% Property Tax Cap
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome the President of the Iowans for Tax Relief Foundation, Chris Ingstad. During the episode Chris and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss Iowa’s new two percent hard cap on property taxes, their efforts lower their income tax, and what’s next for Iowa as they continue...

The post Nebraskanomics: Chris Ingstad on Iowa’s 2% Property Tax Cap appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraska’s Property Tax “Caps” Are Not the Same as Iowa’s
item.description:
In the summer of 2024, Nebraska lawmakers gathered for a special legislative session focused almost entirely on property tax reform. After years of frustration from taxpayers watching valuations and tax bills rise faster than incomes, lawmakers promised meaningful action to slow the growth of property taxes.  The result was LB34 designed to place new limits...

The post Nebraska’s Property Tax “Caps” Are Not the Same as Iowa’s appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] CT AFL-CIO President Warns AI Could be the ‘Nail in the Coffin’ for Democrats
item.description:
Organized labor has found its next target: artificial intelligence.  At a June 1 trade roundtable hosted by U.S. Rep. Rosa DeLauro in New Haven, Connecticut labor leaders warned that AI […]

The post CT AFL-CIO President Warns AI Could be the ‘Nail in the Coffin’ for Democrats  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Inside the MN Department of Education’s literacy teacher training
item.description:
The jury is still out on whether or not the literacy training early-grade teachers receive in Minnesota is actually helping them teach more effectively. Minnesota’s 2023 READ Act set out to reform and update the state’s early literacy education processes. One key reform component was that of mandatory evidence-based literacy instruction training for educators, who...

The post Inside the MN Department of Education’s literacy teacher training appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] California’s “Wealth” Tax is Coming for Everyone
item.description:
If you own property in California, you’re not safe. A new ballot measure will empower the state to confiscate a percentage of the assets of any resident, even though its initial provisions don’t communicate that intent. California’s “One-Time Wealth Tax for State-Funded Healthcare, Education, and Food Assistance Programs Initiative,” which has already qualified for the November ballot,...

The post California’s “Wealth” Tax is Coming for Everyone first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Five Billion Barrels of Crude Oil
item.description:
There is no realistic scenario imaginable that does not include Californians consuming at least another five billion barrels of oil before the state achieves its much touted official goal of a “clean energy future.” Here are the numbers: In 2025, Californians consumed 484 million barrels of crude oil. To understand why another five billion barrels is the...

The post Five Billion Barrels of Crude Oil first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Will students still learn about D-Day under new social studies standards?
item.description:
On June 6, 1944, nearly 160,000 Allied troops stormed the beaches of Normandy, France, pushing back Nazi Germany and changing the course of world history. The D-Day invasion marked the beginning of the end of Adolf Hitler’s regime and was one of the most consequential military operations ever undertaken. This year marks the 82nd anniversary...

The post Will students still learn about D-Day under new social studies standards? appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Jbartlett] BET cuts for the long run
item.description:
As the 2026 legislative session wound down, it looked as though Republicans wouldn’t be able to agree on one of the party’s signature issues: cutting business taxes. During committee of conference negotiations last week, word spread (and was even reported in the news) that a House-passed Business Enterprise Tax cut was dead.  House and Senate […]

The post BET cuts for the long run appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] A Weak Wheat Harvest Should Wake Kansas Up
item.description: Kansas wheat country is facing a hard year. Drought has damaged winter wheat across the Plains, and the latest crop tour estimates point to a much smaller harvest than last year. That should matter to every Kansas policymaker, not just farmers. The 2026 Wheat Quality Council tour projected Kansas wheat yield potential at 38.9 bushels […]

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Uncovering the Iranian Regime Influence Operation to Radicalize Texas Students
item.description:
A Texas Islamist network working to radicalize Shia Muslim students at educational institutions across the state, including Texas public universities, is made up of agents and proxies for the murderous Iranian regime. In March, the Iranian regime and its proxies across the world organized “Al-Quds Day,” a global annual event to celebrate Hezbollah and Iranian...

The post Uncovering the Iranian Regime Influence Operation to Radicalize Texas Students first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Stephanie Smith: Why Alabama must champion the true spirit of civics education and restore the American foundation
item.description:
A recent, jarring exchange on national television perfectly illustrates the cultural and intellectual crisis facing our republic. As reported by The Daily Signal, an MSNBC host expressed utter bewilderment during a broadcast when discussing Speaker of the House Mike Johnson’s assertion that our rights do not derive from the government, but rather from our Creator.

The post Stephanie Smith: Why Alabama must champion the true spirit of civics education and restore the American foundation appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Policy Institute Celebrates June 1st Enactment of Critical Workforce and Free Enterprise Protections
item.description:
The Alabama Policy Institute (API) today marks the implementation of two vital pieces of legislation that enhance economic liberty and expand career opportunities across the state. Championing free enterprise and reforming state regulatory barriers were central priorities established in the 2026 API BluePrint for Alabama.

The post Alabama Policy Institute Celebrates June 1st Enactment of Critical Workforce and Free Enterprise Protections appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Johnlocke] New Harvard journal touts ‘queerness,’ ‘Palestinian liberation’
item.description:
Ira Stoll writes for the Washington Free Beacon about the latest odd development at America’s oldest university. Harvard University—which is being sued by the federal government for antisemitism, has been laying off employees, and is going around telling courts, Congress, and alumni that federal funding cuts are threatening life-saving cancer research—is launching a new publication...

The post New Harvard journal touts ‘queerness,’ ‘Palestinian liberation’ appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Activists push ‘Keep Nine’ amendment to block court packing
item.description:
Shawn Fleetwood writes for the Federalist about a campaign to preserve the current size of the US Supreme Court. When President Trump returned to office last year, Democrat calls to pack the U.S. Supreme Court noticeably went quiet. Like clockwork, the party’s vociferous demands to stack the high court with left-wing activists to produce decisions...

The post Activists push ‘Keep Nine’ amendment to block court packing appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] GOP outsiders ‘rattle’ deep-blue California elections
item.description:
Susan Crabtree writes for Real Clear Politics about two Republicans who fared well in this wek’s California primary elections. In a state that hasn’t elected a Republican governor in nearly two decades and where Democrats outnumber Republicans nearly two to one in registered voters, Tuesday night belonged to two conservative political outsiders who based their...

The post GOP outsiders ‘rattle’ deep-blue California elections appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] Michigan can easily handle adding data centers
item.description: Energy-reduction policies leave a smaller pie for businesses and citizens

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Newsom’s May budget portends future crises for local governments
item.description:
Gov. Jerry Brown’s last budget spent $140.4 billion in the general fund for fiscal year 2018-19. Newsom’s May Revision to his 2026-27 budget exploded that to $246.6 billion. That’s a 76% increase in eight years at a time the state’s population didn’t grow and the Consumer Price Index rose just 32% from Jan. 2019 to […]

The post Newsom’s May budget portends future crises for local governments appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Lawmakers punt on housing supply as prices up 48% since 2019
item.description:
The state’s home prices have jumped 48% while housing for sale has plummeted.

The post Lawmakers punt on housing supply as prices up 48% since 2019 appeared first on Illinois Policy.
